title: Introduction
content:
## Purpose and Scope

The Core Information Standard Version 2.0 defines the fundamental information requirements for person-centred health and care records across all settings in the United Kingdom. This standard ensures consistent capture and sharing of essential demographic, clinical, and social care information to support integrated care delivery.

The standard applies to all health and care professionals, organisations, and systems involved in delivering or coordinating care. It encompasses acute, community, mental health, and social care settings, promoting seamless information exchange across organisational boundaries.

## Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, this standard addresses the critical need for standardised information capture in an increasingly integrated care landscape. It builds upon previous PRSB standards while incorporating lessons learned from implementation experiences and evolving care models. The standard supports the NHS Long Term Plan's vision for joined-up care by ensuring all professionals have access to consistent, comprehensive information about the people they serve.

## Document Overview

This Digital Adaptation Kit provides implementation guidance for the Core Information Standard Version 2.0. It includes clinical terminology mappings, technical specifications, and practical recommendations for system developers and implementers. The kit is organised to support phased implementation, beginning with foundational elements and progressing through increasingly complex information requirements.

title: Health Interventions & Recommendations
content:
## Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Information Governance | Implement robust consent management processes for information sharing, including explicit recording of consent decisions and rationale | Information Governance Alliance | A |
| Reasonable Adjustments | Proactively identify and document all reasonable adjustments required under the Equality Act 2010 to ensure equitable access to services | Equality Act 2010, NHS England | A |
| Safeguarding | Establish clear protocols for recording and sharing safeguarding concerns across organisational boundaries | NHS Safeguarding Accountability Framework | A |
| Mental Capacity | Document all mental capacity assessments with clear decision-specific outcomes and best interests decisions where applicable | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | A |
| Care Planning | Develop person-centred care plans collaboratively with individuals and their representatives, documenting goals, actions, and review arrangements | NICE Guidelines NG86 | B |
| End of Life Care | Record CPR decisions, advance statements, and preferred places of care/death with appropriate review mechanisms | ReSPECT Process, NHS England | A |
| Medication Safety | Maintain comprehensive medication records including allergies, current medications, and discontinuation reasons | NICE Guidelines NG5 | A |

## Implementation Context

These recommendations form the foundation for safe, effective information sharing across health and care settings. Organisations should prioritise implementation based on local risk assessments and population needs, ensuring compliance with legal requirements takes precedence.

title: Generic Personas
content:
## Personas Overview

| Persona ID | Occupational Title | Description | Targeted | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | General Practitioner | Primary care physician responsible for ongoing care coordination and medical management | Yes | Clinical assessment, care coordination, information synthesis, prescribing |
| CISV2.P2 | District Nurse | Community-based nurse providing care in patients' homes | Yes | Clinical procedures, wound care, medication administration, care planning |
| CISV2.P3 | Social Worker | Professional coordinating social care assessments and support packages | Yes | Needs assessment, safeguarding, care package coordination, advocacy |
| CISV2.P4 | Care Home Manager | Responsible for residential care facility operations and resident wellbeing | Yes | Care quality oversight, staff management, regulatory compliance, family liaison |
| CISV2.P5 | Hospital Consultant | Specialist physician managing acute/complex conditions | Yes | Specialist diagnosis, treatment planning, multidisciplinary coordination |
| CISV2.P6 | Mental Health Nurse | Specialist nurse supporting individuals with mental health needs | Yes | Mental health assessment, therapeutic interventions, risk management, medication management |
| CISV2.P7 | Occupational Therapist | Allied health professional supporting functional independence | Yes | Functional assessment, equipment provision, environmental adaptation, rehabilitation planning |
| CISV2.P8 | Pharmacist | Medication expert ensuring safe and effective pharmaceutical care | Yes | Medication review, drug interaction checking, patient counselling, formulary management |
| CISV2.P9 | Paramedic | Emergency care professional providing pre-hospital treatment | Yes | Emergency assessment, life support, clinical decision-making, handover communication |
| CISV2.P10 | Patient/Service User | Individual receiving care and support services | Yes | Self-advocacy, care participation, information provision, consent |

### CISV2.P1 - General Practitioner
GPs serve as the primary point of contact for most healthcare needs, managing ongoing conditions while coordinating specialist referrals. They require comprehensive access to all information sections, particularly medications, problem lists, and care plans.

### CISV2.P2 - District Nurse
District nurses deliver skilled nursing care in community settings, requiring detailed access to clinical procedures, wound care documentation, and medication administration records. They play a crucial role in identifying changes in condition and updating care plans.

### CISV2.P3 - Social Worker
Social workers coordinate complex care packages and safeguarding responses. They need comprehensive access to social circumstances, legal information, and multi-agency plans while ensuring appropriate information governance.

### CISV2.P4 - Care Home Manager
Care home managers oversee daily care delivery for residents with complex needs. They require access to all resident information to ensure safe, person-centred care while managing regulatory requirements.

### CISV2.P5 - Hospital Consultant
Consultants provide specialist expertise for complex conditions. They need efficient access to relevant clinical history, investigation results, and current management plans to inform specialist interventions.

### CISV2.P6 - Mental Health Nurse
Mental health nurses support individuals experiencing mental health challenges. They require access to risk assessments, mental health legislation status, and therapeutic interventions while maintaining therapeutic relationships.

### CISV2.P7 - Occupational Therapist
Occupational therapists assess functional abilities and recommend adaptations. They need access to mobility assessments, equipment provisions, and environmental factors affecting independence.

### CISV2.P8 - Pharmacist
Pharmacists ensure medication safety across care transitions. They require comprehensive medication histories, allergy information, and clinical context to optimise pharmaceutical care.

### CISV2.P9 - Paramedic
Paramedics provide emergency care requiring rapid access to critical information. They need immediate visibility of advance decisions, key medical conditions, and current medications.

### CISV2.P10 - Patient/Service User
Individuals receiving care are central to the information standard. They should have meaningful access to their information, contribute to care planning, and control information sharing preferences.

title: References
content:
## References

### Legislation and Statutory Guidance

1. Equality Act 2010. UK Parliament. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents

2. Mental Capacity Act 2005. UK Parliament. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents

### Clinical Guidelines

10. NICE (2016). Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management (NG56). Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng56

11. NICE (2015). Medicines optimisation (NG5). Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng5

12. NICE (2018). People's experience in adult social care services (NG86). Available at: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng86

### Technical Standards

### Professional Standards

17. Royal College of Physicians (2017). National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2. Available at: https://www.rcplondon.ac.uk/projects/outputs/national-early-warning-score-news-2

18. Resuscitation Council UK (2021). ReSPECT (Recommended Summary Plan for Emergency Care and Treatment). Available at: https://www.resus.org.uk/respect/

title: Core Data Elements
content:
## Core Data Elements

This section provides a comprehensive extraction of all data elements defined in the Core Information Standard Version 2.0. Each element is assigned a unique identifier and documented with its key attributes including description, data type, cardinality, and applicable value sets.

The elements are organised by their functional groupings as defined in the information model, maintaining the hierarchical structure to show relationships between parent groups and child elements.

## Person demographics

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Multiple | Value Set |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | Person demographics | The person's details and contact information. | Group | M | No | — |
| CISV2.DE2 | → Person name | Details of the person's name | Group | M | No | — |
| CISV2.DE3 | → → Person first name | The first name(s) of the person. This includes middle names. | string | M | Yes | NHS Data Dictionary: Person given name |
| CISV2.DE4 | → → Person family name | The family name or surname of the person | string | M | Yes | NHS Data Dictionary: Person family name |
| CISV2.DE5 | → → Person preferred name | The name by which a person wishes to be addressed. The preferred name volunteered by the person or a preferred name given by PDS that the person has asked to be called by. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Person full name |
| CISV2.DE6 | → → Title | Person's title | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Person title |
| CISV2.DE7 | → → Person name suffix | A textual suffix that may be added to the end of a PERSON's name, for example, OBE, MBE, BSc, JP, GM | string | R | Yes | NHS Data Dictionary: Person name suffix |
| CISV2.DE8 | → → Person full name | The full name of the person in text representation. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Patient full name |
| CISV2.DE9 | → Date of birth | The date of birth of the person. | date | M | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Person birth date |
| CISV2.DE10 | → Gender | The person's stated gender. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Person stated gender code |
| CISV2.DE11 | → Ethnicity | The ethnicity of the person as specified by the person. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Ethnic category |
| CISV2.DE12 | → Religion | The religious affiliation as specified by the person. | Coded | R | No | SNOMED CT: <<999000531000000100>> |
| CISV2.DE13 | → Sex | The person's phenotypic sex. Determines how the person will be treated clinically. | string | O | No | NHS Data Dictionary: person phenotypic sex |
| CISV2.DE14 | → NHS number | The unique identifier for a person within the NHS in England and Wales. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: NHS number |
| CISV2.DE15 | → NHS number status indicator | Provides an indication of the reliability of an NHS Number. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: NHS number status indicator |
| CISV2.DE16 | → Other identifier | Country specific or local identifier, e.g. Community Health Index (CHI) in Scotland. | string | R | Yes | NHS Data Dictionary: Local patient identifier(extended) |
| CISV2.DE17 | → Person's address | Person's usual place of residence, and where relevant temporary and correspondence addresses. | Group | M | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE18 | → → Address line 1 | Person's first line of address. | string | M | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address line 1 |
| CISV2.DE19 | → → Address line 2 | Person's second line of address. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address line 2 |
| CISV2.DE20 | → → Address line 3 | Person's third line of address. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address line 3 |
| CISV2.DE21 | → → Address line 4 | Person's fourth line of address. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address line 4 |
| CISV2.DE22 | → → Address line 5 | Person's fifth line of address. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address line 5 |
| CISV2.DE23 | → → Postcode | The person's postcode. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Postcode of usual address |
| CISV2.DE24 | → → Address type | The type of address e.g. Main address, Temp address | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Address association type |
| CISV2.DE25 | → Person's contact details | Details of the person's contact information. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE26 | → → Preferred contact method | Preferred contact method, e.g. email, letter, phone, text message etc. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Communication contact method |
| CISV2.DE27 | → → Person's telephone number details | The person's telephone number details | Group | R | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE28 | → → → Person's telephone number | The person's telephone number | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: UK telephone number |
| CISV2.DE29 | → → → Person's telephone number type | The contact type e.g. home, mobile | string | R | No | FHIR: Contact point use |
| CISV2.DE30 | → → → Preference of telephone number | The preferred order of use for the telephone number | Text | R | No | Free text: Preference of use 1-5, 1 being the highest. |
| CISV2.DE31 | → → Person's email address details | Details of the person's email address | Group | R | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE32 | → → → Person's email address | Email address of the person. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Contact email address |
| CISV2.DE33 | → → → Person's email address type | The type of email address, home, work | string | R | No | FHIR: Contact point use |
| CISV2.DE34 | → → → Preference of email address | The preferred order of use of email address | Text | R | No | Free text: Preference of use 1-5, 1 being the highest. |
| CISV2.DE35 | → → Other contact information | Contact details via fax, sms, url | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Communication contact string |
| CISV2.DE36 | → Photograph | A photograph of the person for identification and to show what the person looks like normally. | identifier | R | No | Multimedia file |
| CISV2.DE37 | → Place of birth | The country of birth of the person. | string | O | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Country code (birth) |
| CISV2.DE38 | → Marital status | An indicator to identify the legal marital status of the person. | string | O | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Person marital status |
| CISV2.DE39 | → Immigration status | Include details of the immigration status of the person, their permissions to live and receive care and support in the UK e.g. asylum seeker, indefinite leave to remain in the UK. Include start date, end date and any comments. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE40 | → → Immigration status | Details of the immigration status of the person, their permissions to live and receive care and support in the UK e.g. asylum seeker, indefinite leave to remain in the UK. | Coded | R | No | Code: 1-10 (various immigration statuses) |
| CISV2.DE41 | → → Start date | Start date of immigration status | datetime | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE42 | → → End date | End date of immigration status | datetime | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE43 | → → Comment | Relevant comments about the person's immigration status | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE44 | → Organ and tissue donation | Whether the person has given consent for organ and/or tissue donation or opted out of automatic donation where applicable. This is information provided by the national register. | boolean | R | No | Flag: Yes or No |
| CISV2.DE45 | → Overseas visitor status | The charging category relating to an overseas visitor. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Overseas visitor charging category |

## GP practice

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Multiple | Value Set |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE46 | GP practice | Details of the person's GP practice. | Group | M | No | — |
| CISV2.DE47 | → GP practice record entry | This is a GP practice record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items. | Group | R | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE48 | → → GP name | The name of the person's GP. If the person is registered with a GP practice, their usual GP name will be something volunteered by the person or their representative. | PRSB-Standards/WRIST-ips-datatypes | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE49 | → → GP practice details | Name and address of the person's registered GP Practice. Registered GP practice details are available from the Person Demographics Service (PDS), or volunteered from the person or their representative. Include details of the practice name and address. | PRSB-Standards/WRIST-ips-datatypes | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE50 | → → GP practice identifier | The identifier of the registered GP practice. | PRSB-Standards/WRIST-ips-datatypes | M | No | NHS Data Dictionary: GENERAL MEDICAL PRACTICE (PATIENT REGISTRATION) |

## About me

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Multiple | Value Set |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE51 | About me | About me | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE52 | → What is most important to me | A description of what is most important to you. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE53 | → → What is most important to me | A description of what is most important to you. Emergency Information: Include any essential information that any professional in health and social care should know about the you in any situation, including emergencies. Other Information: This could include: Values, Spirituality/religion, Ethnicity, Culture, Pets, Goals and aspirations, Meaningful activities including leisure activities, visiting places, sport and exercise, listening to music, employment, education, volunteering | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE54 | → People who are important to me | Details of who is important to you and why. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE55 | → → People who are important to me | Details of who is important to you and why. They could be family members, carers, friends, members of staff etc. Include how you want the people important to you to be engaged and involved in your care and support in both emergency and normal situations. Include how you stay connected to the people important to you. Who should not be contacted or consulted about your care and support and why, if you wish to say | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE56 | → How I communicate and how to communicate with me | A description of how you communicate normally. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE57 | → → How I communicate and how to communicate with me | A description of how you communicate normally including any communication aids you use, for example a hearing aid. Include your preferred language of communication, if your first language is not English. Include how you would communicate when you are in pain or distress. Include how you communicate choices. Include how you give feedback or raise a concern. Include how you like to receive information. Describe how you would like others to engage and communicate with you, including how you would like to be addressed. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE58 | → My wellness | A description covering what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel on a typical day through to on a day when you are unwell or really unwell. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE59 | → → My wellness | A description covering what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel on a typical day through to on a day when you are unwell or really unwell. Include any causes that might result in you becoming unwell and strategies for avoiding or addressing the causes. For example, not drinking enough water could cause constipation. Include any signs that indicate you might be becoming unwell. On a bad day describe what is different about what you are able to do, how you engage with others and how you feel. Include how your everyday life is affected by any medical conditions e.g. dementia and any symptoms e.g. itchiness, cough, pain and how you manage those conditions. Include past health issues or experiences that need to be considered. Include your wellbeing and lifestyle goals and aspirations | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE60 | → Please do and please don't | A description of things you want someone supporting you to do (or not to do). | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE61 | → → Please do and please don't | A description of things you want someone supporting you to do (or not to do). Things you want someone to do might include (for example): Talk to me not to my carer, remind me to take my medication, encourage me to wash my hands regularly, explain to me what is happening and why, respond to my communication. A description of things you do not want someone supporting you to do might include (for example): Discussing or asking questions about certain topics, making assumptions about something, providing support when it is not wanted, talking to you in a certain way, undergoing a specific intervention or taking a medication the person does not want. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE62 | → How and when to support me | A description of how and when you want someone caring for you to support you. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE63 | → → How and when to support me | A description of how and when you want someone caring for you to support you. This could include support needs in an emergency situation (for example taking blood). This could include support you need to maintain important routines or to carry out particular activities, for example: Personal care routines, Eating and drinking, Bedtime routines, Taking medications, Moving and transitioning. This could also include support needed with: wearing glasses/hearing aids/false teeth etc, making informed choices or understanding dangers and risks, managing your emotions, moods and behaviours, memory or confusion. Include how your support needs change in different environments. Include any triggers that might result in you needing further support and strategies for avoiding or addressing the triggers. Include how you want the support to be provided. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE64 | → Also worth knowing about me | A description of what is also worth knowing about you for people caring or supporting you. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE65 | → → Also worth knowing about me | A description of what is also worth knowing about you for people caring or supporting you. This could include a short history of your life (where you have worked, where you lived, important events in your life, important people in your past life). This could include a short profile of your current life: your work / study, your aspirations, your skills, your networks, things you like e.g. particular foods, places, a football team and things you like to talk about, things you dislike. This could also include any care and support preferences that have not been included elsewhere. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE66 | → Date | This is a record of the date that this information was last updated. | datetime | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE67 | → Supported to write this by | Where relevant, this is a record of name, relationship/role and contact details of the individual who supported the person to write this section e.g. carer, family member, advocate, professional. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE68 | → Location to multimedia file | The link to the person's multimedia file | text | R | No | Link to URL |

## Individual requirements

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Multiple | Value Set |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE69 | Individual requirements | The individual requirements of the person. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE70 | → Reasonable adjustment | A record of reasonable adjustments that must be provided by the service to comply with the Equality Act 2010. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE71 | → → Reasonable adjustment record entry | This is a reasonable adjustment record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items | Group | R | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE72 | → → → Location | The location where the reasonable adjustment was identified. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE73 | → → → → Coded value | The coded value for location. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Organisation data service code |
| CISV2.DE74 | → → → → Free text | Free text field to be used if no code is available | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE75 | → → → Reasonable adjustment flag | Under the Equality Act 2010, organisations have a legal duty to make changes in their approach or provision to ensure that services are as accessible to people with disabilities as they are for everybody else. These changes are called reasonable adjustments. Reasonable adjustments can mean alterations to buildings by providing lifts, wide doors, ramps and tactile signage, but may also mean changes to policies, procedures and staff training to ensure that services work equally well for people with physical or sensory disabilities, learning disabilities or long-term conditions such as dementia. Other adjustments include longer appointments, providing easy read materials or communications via a carer. This legal duty is anticipatory which means a service should know about a person's need for adjustments when they are referred or present for care. For this to happen, and for optimum care to be delivered, adjustments need to be recorded and shared across the NHS. | boolean | R | No | SNOMED CT: 1108111000000107 |Requires reasonable adjustments for health and care access (Equality Act 2010) (finding)| |
| CISV2.DE76 | → → → Reasonable adjustment | The reasonable adjustment required e.g. requires specific contact method, requires communication professional | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE77 | → → → → Coded value | The coded value for reasonable adjustment | Coded | R | No | SNOMED CT: Accessible information standard refsets |
| CISV2.DE78 | → → → → Free text | Free text field to be used if no code is available | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE79 | → → → Reasonable adjustment additional detail | Further detail about the support required and the consequence of not providing it. | string | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE80 | → → → Date | The date the reasonable adjustment was identified. | datetime | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE81 | → → → Performing professional | The professional who confirmed the need for the reasonable adjustment. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE82 | → → → → Name | The name of the professional. | string | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE83 | → → → → Role | The role the professional has in relation to the person. | Coded | R | No | FHIR: sdsjobrole |
| CISV2.DE84 | → → → → Grade | The grade of the professional. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE85 | → → → → Speciality | The specialty of the professional. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Main specialty code |
| CISV2.DE86 | → → → → Professional identifier | Professional identifier for the professional e.g. GMC number, HCPC number etc or the personal identifier used by the local organisation. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Professional registration identifier |
| CISV2.DE87 | → → → → Organisation | The name of the organisation the professional works for. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE88 | → → → → Contact details | Contact details of the professional | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: UK phone number |
| CISV2.DE89 | → → → Person completing record | A record of the details of the person that entered the reasonable adjustment and the date on which it was entered. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE90 | → → → → Name | The name of the person completing the record. | string | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE91 | → → → → Role | The organisational role of the person completing record. | Coded | R | No | FHIR: SDSjobrole |
| CISV2.DE92 | → → → → Grade | The grade of the person completing the record. | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE93 | → → → → Specialty | The main specialty of the person completing the record. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Activity treatment function code |
| CISV2.DE94 | → → → → Organisation | The organisation the person completing the record works for. | Coded | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Organisation data service |
| CISV2.DE95 | → → → → Professional identifier | Professional identifier for the person completing the record e.g. GMC number, HCPC number etc or the personal identifier used by the local organisation. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Professional registration identifier |
| CISV2.DE96 | → → → → Date completed | The date and time the record was completed. | datetime | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE97 | → → → → Contact details | Contact details of the person completing the record. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: UK telephone number |
| CISV2.DE98 | → Impairment | A record of any impairments for the person relating to reasonable adjustments. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE99 | → → Impairment record entry | This is an impairment record entry. There may be 0 to many record entries under a section. Each record entry is made up of a number of elements or data items | Group | R | Yes | — |
| CISV2.DE100 | → → → Location | The location where the impairment was identified. | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE101 | → → → → Coded value | The coded value for location. | string | R | No | NHS Data Dictionary: Organisation code service |
| CISV2.DE102 | → → → → Free text | Free text field to be used if no code is available | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE103 | → → → Impairment category | The category of the impairment e.g. Autism, Dementia, Physical disability, Sensory disability - such as sight or hearing, Long-term condition | Group | R | No | — |
| CISV2.DE104 | → → → → Coded value | The coded value for impairment. | Coded | R | No | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE105 | → → → → Free text | Free text field to be used if no code is available | text | R | No | Free text |
| CISV2.DE106 | → → → Impairment additional detail | Description of what the person can do with the impairment, what they want to be able to do and how they should be supported to do it and what the consequences are of not providing the support. title: Business Processes & Workflows
content:
The Core Information Standard Version 2.0 supports essential business processes across health and care settings. These workflows ensure consistent information capture and sharing to enable integrated person-centred care delivery. Each process maps key activities performed by health and care professionals, identifying data requirements and interdependencies.

The following processes represent common patterns of care delivery, from initial patient registration through ongoing care coordination. Each workflow identifies the personas involved, data elements accessed or collected, and decision points that guide care pathways.

processes_data.summary_markdown: Essential workflows supporting integrated health and care delivery across all settings.

activities.title: Patient Registration and Demographic Update
activities.personas: General Practitioner, District Nurse, Care Home Manager
activities.objectives: Ensure accurate patient identification and demographic information for safe care delivery

activities.title: Medication Review and Reconciliation
activities.objectives: Ensure medication safety through comprehensive review and accurate reconciliation

activities.title: Care Plan Development and Review
activities.personas: Social Worker, Occupational Therapist, Mental Health Nurse, Patient/Service User
activities.objectives: Create person-centred care plans addressing individual goals and support needs

activities.title: Emergency Response Coordination
activities.objectives: Rapid access to critical information for emergency care decisions

activities.title: Discharge Planning and Coordination
activities.personas: Hospital Consultant, Social Worker, Care Home Manager, District Nurse
activities.objectives: Safe and timely discharge with appropriate support arrangements

title: User Scenarios
content:
## Emergency Admission with Complex Needs

Mrs. Elizabeth Thompson, an 82-year-old woman with multiple comorbidities including diabetes, heart failure, and mild cognitive impairment, is brought to the Emergency Department following a fall at home. The paramedic team needs immediate access to her medical history, current medications, and any advance decisions to provide appropriate care.

**Personas involved:** Paramedic, Hospital Consultant, District Nurse

**Linked processes:** CISV2.D

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE14 | NHS number | Patient identification and record retrieval |
| CISV2.DE2 | Person name | Patient verification |
| CISV2.DE70 | Reasonable adjustment | Identifying communication needs due to cognitive impairment |
| CISV2.DE52 | What is most important to me | Understanding patient preferences during emergency care |
| CISV2.DE56 | How I communicate and how to communicate with me | Adapting communication approach |

## Medication Review in Community Setting

Mr. James Wilson, a 75-year-old man with polypharmacy (taking 8 regular medications), attends his GP practice for a routine medication review. The pharmacist needs to reconcile his current medications, check for interactions, and ensure optimal therapy while considering his kidney function and recent hospital discharge.

**Personas involved:** Pharmacist, General Practitioner

**Linked processes:** CISV2.B

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE14 | NHS number | Patient identification |
| CISV2.DE46 | GP practice | Confirming registered practice |
| CISV2.DE48 | GP name | Identifying responsible GP |
| CISV2.DE9 | Date of birth | Age-appropriate prescribing |
| CISV2.DE10 | Gender | Gender-specific medication considerations |

## Social Care Assessment for Independent Living

Mrs. Priya Patel, a 68-year-old woman living alone, has been referred for a social care assessment following concerns raised by her daughter about her ability to manage daily activities. The social worker needs to conduct a comprehensive assessment considering her cultural needs, home environment, and support network.

**Personas involved:** Social Worker, Occupational Therapist, Patient/Service User

**Linked processes:** CISV2.C

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE17 | Person's address | Assessing home environment and location |
| CISV2.DE11 | Ethnicity | Understanding cultural considerations |
| CISV2.DE12 | Religion | Considering religious practices in care planning |
| CISV2.DE54 | People who are important to me | Identifying support network |
| CISV2.DE148 | Mobility | Assessing physical capabilities |
| CISV2.DE173 | Other individual requirements | Capturing specific needs |

## Hospital Discharge to Care Home

Mr. George Brown, an 89-year-old man with advanced dementia, is being discharged from hospital to a care home following treatment for pneumonia. Multiple professionals need to coordinate to ensure safe transfer and continuity of care.

**Personas involved:** Hospital Consultant, Social Worker, Care Home Manager, District Nurse

**Linked processes:** CISV2.E

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE125 | Cognition | Documenting cognitive status for care home |
| CISV2.DE69 | Individual requirements | Identifying all support needs |
| CISV2.DE98 | Impairment | Recording functional impairments |
| CISV2.DE60 | Please do and please don't | Providing care preferences to new setting |
| CISV2.DE62 | How and when to support me | Guiding care home staff |

## Mental Health Crisis Response

Ms. Sarah Johnson, a 34-year-old woman with bipolar disorder, presents in mental health crisis at A&E. The mental health nurse needs to assess her current state, review her care plan, and determine appropriate intervention while ensuring her safety.

**Personas involved:** Mental Health Nurse, Hospital Consultant

**Linked processes:** CISV2.D

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE14 | NHS number | Accessing mental health records |
| CISV2.DE70 | Reasonable adjustment | Understanding specific mental health accommodations |
| CISV2.DE58 | My wellness | Identifying baseline functioning and triggers |
| CISV2.DE64 | Also worth knowing about me | Understanding personal context |

## Routine GP Consultation with Multiple Conditions

Mr. David Chen, a 65-year-old man with diabetes, hypertension, and recently diagnosed COPD, attends his GP for routine monitoring. The GP needs to review all conditions, adjust medications, and coordinate care with specialist services.

**Personas involved:** General Practitioner, Patient/Service User

**Linked processes:** CISV2.A

**Data elements involved:**

| Element ID | Label | Used For |
|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE14 | NHS number | Patient identification |
| CISV2.DE9 | Date of birth | Age-related clinical decisions |
| CISV2.DE46 | GP practice | Confirming registration |
| CISV2.DE27 | Person's telephone number details | Updating contact information |
| CISV2.DE31 | Person's email address details | Providing digital communication options |

scenarios_metadata.title: Social Care Assessment for Independent Living
Routine GP Consultation with Multiple Conditions

title: Decision-Support Logic
content:
## Decision-Support Logic

The following decision tables define key clinical and operational logic within the Core Information Standard to support consistent, evidence-based decision-making across care settings.

### Table 1: Reasonable Adjustment Identification

**Description:** Determines when reasonable adjustments should be flagged and documented based on identified impairments or individual requirements.

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Impairment category identified | CISV2.DE103 (Impairment category - Coded value) | Set CISV2.DE75 (Reasonable adjustment flag) = TRUE |
| Communication impairment present | CISV2.DE103 = "Communication impairment" | Populate CISV2.DE77 (Reasonable adjustment - Coded value) with appropriate communication support code |
| Mobility impairment present | CISV2.DE153 (Mobility needs - Coded value) populated | Set CISV2.DE75 = TRUE and document mobility accommodations |
| Cognitive impairment identified | CISV2.DE130 (Cognition) populated | Set CISV2.DE75 = TRUE and specify cognitive support requirements |
| Sensory impairment documented | CISV2.DE103 = "Sensory disability" | Set CISV2.DE75 = TRUE and detail sensory accommodations needed |

### Table 2: Safeguarding Alert Triggers

**Description:** Identifies conditions that should trigger safeguarding assessments or alerts.

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Safeguarding indicator = TRUE | Alert element (Safeguarding indicator) = Y | Generate safeguarding alert to care team |
| Looked after child status active | Looked after child start date populated AND end date empty | Flag for social services coordination |
| Mental capacity assessment indicates lack of capacity | Mental capacity assessment outcome = "Lacks capacity" | Review consent arrangements and best interests decisions |
| DoLS authorised | DoLS application status = "Authorised" | Ensure care plan reflects DoLS conditions |
| Child protection plan active | Child protection plan start date populated AND end date empty | Priority safeguarding coordination required |

### Table 3: Emergency Information Priority

**Description:** Determines which information elements must be immediately accessible during emergency care.

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Emergency admission | Process = CISV2.D | Display: CISV2.DE14 (NHS number), CISV2.DE70 (Reasonable adjustments), Allergies section, Problem list, Current medications |
| CPR decision exists | CPR decision populated | Prominently display CPR status (CISV2.DE450-456) |
| Advance decision present | ADRT section populated | Flag advance decisions for clinical team |
| Mental Health Act status | MHA status populated | Display legal status and restrictions |
| Communication needs identified | CISV2.DE56 (How I communicate) populated | Show communication requirements prominently |

### Table 4: Medication Reconciliation Logic

**Description:** Supports medication safety through systematic reconciliation processes.

| Condition | Input Data Elements | Output Action |
|---|---|---|
| Admission medication review | Process = CISV2.B AND Location = "Hospital" | Compare current medications with GP records; flag discrepancies |
| Allergy/adverse reaction recorded | Causative agent (medication) matches current prescription | Generate contraindication alert |
| Medication status = "stopped" | Medication discontinued entry populated | Update active medication list; document reason |
| Polypharmacy threshold exceeded | Count of active medications ≥ 8 | Trigger comprehensive medication review |
| High-risk medication prescribed | Medication name matches high-risk drug list | Require additional safety checks and monitoring plan |

title: Indicators & Performance Metrics
content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

The following indicators support quality monitoring and performance improvement for Core Information Standard implementation.

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.IND.01 | Reasonable Adjustment Documentation Rate | Count of records with CISV2.DE75 (Reasonable adjustment flag) = TRUE AND CISV2.DE77 (Reasonable adjustment details) populated | Total records with CISV2.DE103 (Impairment category) populated | By organisation, impairment type, age group | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.02 | About Me Completion Rate | Count of records with ≥3 populated subsections in CISV2.DE51-68 (About me section) | Total active patient records | By care setting, age group, length of care | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.03 | Consent Documentation Completeness | Records with consent for information sharing (Legal information section) documented with date and decision | Total records | By organisation, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.04 | Safeguarding Response Time | Records where time between safeguarding indicator = TRUE and first safeguarding action < 24 hours | Total records with safeguarding indicator = TRUE | By organisation, safeguarding type | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.05 | Care Plan Currency | Records with care plan (Care and support plan section) reviewed within specified timeframe | Total records with active care plans | By care setting, primary support reason | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.06 | Emergency Information Availability | Records with populated: NHS number (CISV2.DE14), allergies section, problem list, current medications | Total emergency admissions (Process CISV2.D) | By admission source, time of day | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.07 | Advance Decision Recording | Records with ADRT or advance statement documented | Total records for patients aged ≥65 OR with life-limiting conditions | By age group, diagnosis category | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.08 | Professional Contact Completeness | Records with ≥1 professional contact (Professional contacts section) with name, role, and contact details | Total active care records | By care setting, complexity score | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.09 | Risk Assessment Documentation | Records with current risk assessment (Risks section with date within 6 months) | Total records flagged as requiring risk assessment | By risk type, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.10 | Medication Reconciliation Completion | Admission records with documented medication reconciliation within 24 hours | Total admissions (Process CISV2.E) | By admission type, day of week | Monthly |
| CISV2.IND.11 | Social Context Recording | Records with populated household composition OR accommodation status OR social circumstances | Total records | By age group, care setting | Quarterly |
| CISV2.IND.12 | Communication Needs Identification | Records with CISV2.DE56 (How I communicate) populated for patients with CISV2.DE103 indicating communication impairment | Total records with communication impairment | By impairment type, organisation | Monthly |

title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Linked Processes | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR.01 | Patient Registration & Demographics | System SHALL support creation and update of patient records with all mandatory demographic elements (CISV2.DE1-50) including NHS number validation | CISV2.A | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.02 | Reasonable Adjustment Management | System SHALL flag and document reasonable adjustments per Equality Act 2010, supporting all adjustment categories in CISV2.DE70-195 | CISV2.A, CISV2.C, CISV2.E | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.03 | Multi-professional Access Control | System SHALL provide role-based access supporting all personas (CISV2.P1-P10) with appropriate information visibility | All processes | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.04 | Emergency Information Display | System SHALL present critical information (allergies, CPR status, key conditions) within 3 clicks during emergency access | CISV2.D | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.05 | Medication Reconciliation Workflow | System SHALL support systematic medication review comparing current medications across care settings with conflict detection | CISV2.B | High |
| CISV2.FR.06 | Care Plan Versioning | System SHALL maintain version history of care plans with ability to track changes, review dates, and responsible professionals | CISV2.C | High |
| CISV2.FR.07 | Consent Management | System SHALL record and enforce information sharing consent decisions including best interests determinations | All processes | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.08 | Safeguarding Alerts | System SHALL generate automatic alerts when safeguarding indicators are triggered with escalation workflows | CISV2.C, CISV2.D | Critical |
| CISV2.FR.09 | Cross-Setting Information Exchange | System SHALL support bidirectional data exchange using standard APIs between acute, primary, and social care systems | CISV2.E | High |
| CISV2.FR.10 | Patient-Contributed Information | System SHALL allow patient/carer input for "About Me" sections with appropriate validation and professional review | CISV2.A, CISV2.C | Medium |
| CISV2.FR.11 | Advance Decision Recording | System SHALL support structured capture of ADRT, advance statements, and end-of-life preferences with prominence flags | All processes | High |
| CISV2.FR.12 | Audit Trail Generation | System SHALL maintain comprehensive audit logs of all access, modifications, and consent overrides with user attribution | All processes | Critical |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Classification | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR.01 | Response Time - Emergency Access | Emergency information retrieval SHALL complete within 2 seconds for 95% of requests | Performance | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.02 | System Availability | System SHALL maintain 99.9% availability during core hours (8am-8pm) with 99.5% overall | Availability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.03 | Data Encryption | All patient data SHALL be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3 minimum) | Security | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.04 | Concurrent User Support | System SHALL support minimum 1000 concurrent users per organisation without performance degradation | Scalability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.05 | Mobile Responsiveness | Interface SHALL be fully functional on tablets and mobile devices for community care scenarios | Usability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.06 | Accessibility Compliance | System SHALL meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards supporting users with disabilities | Accessibility | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.07 | Backup & Recovery | System SHALL maintain hourly backups with recovery point objective (RPO) of 1 hour and recovery time objective (RTO) of 4 hours | Reliability | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.08 | Integration Standards | System SHALL support HL7 FHIR R4 UK Core for all data exchange with response times <5 seconds | Interoperability | High |
| CISV2.NFR.09 | Clinical Safety Compliance | System SHALL comply with DCB0129 and DCB0160 clinical safety standards with documented hazard log | Safety | Critical |
| CISV2.NFR.10 | Session Management | System SHALL enforce session timeout after 20 minutes of inactivity with secure session resumption | Security | High |
| CISV2.NFR.11 | Search Performance | Patient search SHALL return results within 3 seconds for databases up to 10 million records | Performance | High |
| CISV2.NFR.12 | Data Retention Compliance | System SHALL support configurable retention periods per record type in line with NHS Records Management Code | Compliance | Critical |
